opp.name: Aeroméxico S2P via EY LATAM
opp.next_step: 6/22 MC – Sent follow-up email to Erika Baños and initiated direct outreach to Mikel Piña. Engaged EY to better understand current client prioritization and whether their ongoing finance initiative with Aeroméxico is impacting momentum on the procurement workstream. Continuing a dual-track engagement strategy through both EY and direct client outreach. Shared new Spanish-language Zycus resources, including the Spanish landing page and aviation collateral, to increase stakeholder engagement and maintain visibility while awaiting meeting confirmation. Risk: limited client responsiveness and unclear prioritization of procurement initiative relative to other transformation projects.
opp.ais_why:
• Zycus has flagged AI importance as 'Don't Know' - the only opportunity in this batch where the rep could not confirm AI relevance. That signals the buyer has not surfaced AI as a priority in any captured interaction.
• The opportunity description frames Aeroméxico's procurement transformation as EY-led, with scope covering intake, spend analytics, sourcing and potential CLM integration. That reads as functional procurement modernization rather than AI-first transformation. Merlin, ANA and Agentic AI are tagged but appear to be vendor positioning rather than buyer-requested.
• There are zero Avoma calls at opportunity or account level across the date range. The deal has stalled - the May 2026 next step is to re-establish alignment and restart momentum - which suggests the project is not progressing and certainly not propelled by AI urgency. Confidence is low because there is no recorded buyer voice.

sources: SF Products__c: iContract;iSource;Merlin;Merlin Intake;Agentic AI;ANA (product scope)
SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c: no data
Avoma 12 May 2026 demo: not recorded, no transcript
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case is documented. The product scope (iContract, iSource, Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA per SF Products__c) signals a full S2P+AI+contracting transformation, but no Salesforce field (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c) carries a buyer-stated pain, savings target, cycle-time improvement, or cost baseline. The single recorded touchpoint (12 May 2026 demo) has no transcript so we cannot confirm whether the buyer articulated pain on that call. Without a quantified value case the Economic Buyer has no commercial anchor and the deal cannot advance to commercials or negotiation. The immediate move is to run a discovery session with the VP Planning and SVP Supply Chain to map their current-state pain (spend visibility, contract cycle time, supplier risk, manual processes) and quantify the impact in terms they can defend internally (cost, FTE, cycle days, error rate).

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Erika Baños (VP Planning), SVP Supply Chain (no evidence of advocacy)
SF X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c: no data
Avoma 12 May 2026 demo: not recorded, no follow-up engagement
SF Next Step 22 Jun 2026: rep outreach, no confirmed buyer reply (limited responsiveness)
champion.narrative: No champion is confirmed. The two contact roles (Erika Baños VP Planning, SVP Supply Chain) are early-stage operational contacts, but there is no evidence of advocacy, internal selling, or access to the Economic Buyer. The 12 May demo (not recorded, 45 days ago) is the only touchpoint; no subsequent call or meeting has occurred. Rep follow-up email to the VP Planning and direct outreach to the SVP Supply Chain on 22 Jun (4 days ago) have received no confirmed reply per Next Step, signaling limited responsiveness. No Salesforce field (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c) names a champion, and no call has shown a buyer stakeholder introducing us to others, sharing internal docs, or pushing back on competitors for us. The immediate move is to restart the relationship with a high-value, low-ask soft touch (a Spanish-language aviation ROI case study or Merlin demo video) within 7 days, then on the first live call ask the SVP Supply Chain who internally is driving this initiative and who we should partner with to build the internal business case.

sources: SF Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c: no data
SF How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c: no data
Avoma 12 May 2026 demo: not recorded, no transcript
SF Next Step, Tasks: no competitor mentioned
competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source. Salesforce Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c are empty, no call recorded competitive discussion, and no Next Step or Task mentions a rival. The 12 May demo (not recorded) provides no transcript evidence of competitive benchmarking. With no competitor data and no buyer engagement to surface it, we do not know if this is a sole-source EY-led evaluation, a multi-vendor RFP, or a do-nothing/status-quo risk. The wedge and win strategy cannot be tuned without knowing the alternative. The immediate move is to ask the SVP Supply Chain on the next live call whether they are evaluating other vendors, what their current procurement/contracting stack is (incumbent to displace), and how they are addressing the problem today (manual, legacy system, or another vendor).

sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Compelling_Event__c: no data
SF Products__c: S2P + contracting + AI (scope, not pain)
Avoma 12 May 2026 demo: not recorded, no transcript
SF Next Step 22 Jun 2026: finance initiative may be deprioritizing procurement
identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. No Salesforce field (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Compelling_Event__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c) carries buyer-stated pain, and the 12 May demo (not recorded) provides no transcript. The product scope (S2P, contracting, AI intake, analytics) implies they want to modernize procurement, but we do not know the current-state pain (manual POs? fragmented supplier data? slow contract cycles? no spend visibility? high maverick spend?), the pain owner, or what happens if they do nothing. Next Step 22 Jun notes a competing finance transformation initiative that may be deprioritizing procurement, suggesting procurement pain may not be urgent enough to secure budget vs finance priorities. The immediate move is to run a discovery call with the VP Planning and SVP Supply Chain to surface their top 3 pain points, quantify the cost/impact of each, and identify a compelling event (audit, cost-reduction mandate, supplier-risk incident, contract expiration) that makes this urgent.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting and procurement mechanics are unknown. No Salesforce field (Current_Contract_Expiration__c, What_is_the_decision_process__c) describes MSA/SOW requirements, legal review cycles, InfoSec questionnaire, or procurement channel (direct vs EY-led). EY LATAM is named in the opp title and attended the 12 May demo, but whether they will be implementation partner, reseller, or both is undefined, and whether they influence vendor selection is unclear per Next Step 22 Jun. No call has discussed contracting timeline, redlining process, or InfoSec. The immediate move is to ask the SVP Supply Chain (on the next live call) whether this will be a direct Zycus contract or EY-resold, what legal/InfoSec/procurement gates exist, and what the contracting timeline looks like once pricing is agreed.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Erika Baños (VP Planning), SVP Supply Chain (operational, not EB)
SF X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c: no data
SF Account contacts: CTO Alicia Berbel, COO Santiago Diago (not engaged on opp)
Avoma 12 May 2026 demo: no transcript
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified. The two contact roles (Erika Baños VP Planning and Purchasing Inventory Supply, and SVP Supply Chain) are operational/supply-chain leaders, not C-level budget holders. A $200k S2P+AI transformation at an airline requires CFO, COO, or C-level Digital Transformation sponsorship, none of whom are mapped. No Salesforce field (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) names a budget authority, and no call recorded commercial or pricing conversation. The single touchpoint (12 May demo, not recorded) lacks transcript evidence of EB participation. The account contact list shows a CTO (Alicia Berbel) and COO (Santiago Diago) who are not yet engaged on this opp. The immediate move is to ask the SVP Supply Chain (on the next live call) who will approve the $200k investment and what the approval process looks like, then secure an introduction to that C-level sponsor within 14 days.

sources: SF What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c: no data
Avoma 12 May 2026 demo attendees: 2 Aeroméxico, 3 EY, 3 Zycus (EY present, role unclear)
SF Next Step 22 Jun 2026: dual-track strategy (EY + direct), EY role undefined
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is undefined. No Salesforce field (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c) describes the approval chain, and no call has mapped the buying committee or gate sequence. The single touchpoint (12 May demo, attendees: 2 Aeroméxico contacts, 3 EY consultants, 3 Zycus) suggests EY LATAM is involved, but their role (implementation partner only vs decision influence, and whether they hold a vendor preference) is unclear per Next Step 22 Jun. The rep noted a dual-track engagement strategy (EY + direct client) but decision authority path is undefined. No CFO, COO, or procurement head has joined a call. The immediate move is to ask the SVP Supply Chain on the next live call: who is on the buying committee, what are the gate steps (demo, RFI, shortlist, pricing, contract), who has final sign-off, and what is EY's role in the decision.

decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria are documented. The buyer has not articulated evaluation criteria, scoring rubric, or RFP/RFI requirements. No Salesforce field (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c, Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c) carries criteria, and the 12 May demo (not recorded) provides no transcript evidence of use-case prioritization or scoring discussion. The product scope (iContract, iSource, Merlin, ANA) suggests they want S2P, contracting, AI intake, and analytics, but we do not know what matters most (ease-of-use for Spanish-speaking users? Aviation-specific supplier risk? Contract redlining speed? Merlin ROI?) or how they will score vendors. The immediate move is to run a structured discovery workshop with the VP Planning and SVP Supply Chain to map their top 3 to 5 business requirements, confirm must-haves vs nice-to-haves, and ask how they will score vendors (and whether this is an RFP, shortlist bake-off, or sole-source evaluation).

Close date 2026-12-31 is 188 days out but is a placeholder with no buyer-validated timeline or compelling event. Last verified Salesforce activity 22 Jun 2026 (rep outreach, not buyer engagement). One touchpoint discovered (12 May 2026 demo, not recorded, 45 days ago). No subsequent buyer call or meeting. Rep reached out 22 Jun via email to Erika Baños and directly to SVP Supply Chain, and engaged EY to clarify prioritization, but no buyer reply confirmed. Deal is cold; close date is fiction.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains cold 144+ days in Formal Evaluation with zero buyer engagement. Rep outreach 22-Jun to VP and SVP via EY awaits response; last demo was 12-May, unrecorded. No timeline, no milestones, no validated need—deal appears dead.

flags: IMMEDIATE RE-ENGAGEMENT WITH the relevant stakeholder: 4 days since rep outreach (22 Jun follow-up to the relevant stakeholderños, direct reach to the relevant stakeholderña) with no confirmed reply. Limited client responsiveness is flagged as a risk. The highest-leverage move is to secure a live call with the relevant stakeholder (the likely budget authority) in the next 7 days to extract pain, timeline, and decision process, converting a cold email thread into an active relationship. If no reply by 03 Jul (10 business days), escalate through EY to request a joint client meeting and assess whether the deal is truly live or should be disqualified.

CLARIFY EY ROLE AND PRIORITIZATION: Next Step notes EY was engaged 22 Jun to understand whether Aeroméxico's ongoing finance transformation is delaying the procurement workstream. This is critical path: if procurement is deprioritized or budget-starved by the finance initiative, the 2026-12-31 close is fiction. Within the next 14 days, align with EY on: (1) Aeroméxico's current initiative prioritization and budget allocation, (2) EY's implementation timeline and SOW scoping status, (3) whether EY holds decision influence or is pure implementation overlay, and (4) EY's view on competitive landscape (sole-source vs RFP). Use EY as a forcing function to get in front of the Economic Buyer if direct outreach continues to stall.

Competing finance transformation initiative may be deprioritizing procurement workstream and starving it of budget/exec attention per Next Step 22 Jun. Procurement vs finance prioritization is unclear. Win lever: on next live call ask the relevant stakeholder directly whether procurement initiative is alive, what the budget/timeline is, and how it relates to the finance transformation (parallel, sequential, or competing for same dollars). If procurement is deprioritized, qualify out or defer to 2027.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is cold (144+ days in Formal Evaluation, no milestone progression, zero recorded buyer engagement in 45 days). Rep outreach 22 Jun awaits reply. Competing finance initiative may be starving procurement of budget/exec attention. No C-level sponsor, no champion, no MEDDPICC coverage, single-thread risk (2 contacts). Immediate win-strategy levers: (1) restart the relationship with a high-value soft touch within 7 days, (2) surface whether procurement initiative is alive vs deprioritized, (3) multi-thread to C-level (CTO, COO) and Digital Transformation contacts to find the real sponsor.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape is completely unknown. No competitors named in any Salesforce field, zero buyer calls, and Next Step silent on alternatives. It is unclear whether Aeroméxico is running a competitive RFP, sole-sourcing through EY, replacing an incumbent S2P system, or comparing Zycus to a do-nothing scenario. Without competition visibility, differentiation and wedge strategy cannot be tailored. The immediate move is to ask EY and the buyer stakeholders explicitly who else is being evaluated and why.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI scope is strong (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA per SF Products__c), but no AIS score or call evidence exists to validate buyer AI appetite. No recorded conversation about Merlin ROI, agentic negotiation, or AI use-cases. Spanish Zycus resources (landing page, aviation collateral) shared 22 Jun per Next Step, a positive signal for localized AI positioning. However, with zero recorded buyer engagement and no MEDDPICC coverage, AI positioning strength cannot be assessed. Score: unknown. Under-positioned risk: high, because the AI story has not been told on a recorded call and we have no evidence the buyer understands Merlin value.

Spanish-language support is a positive signal (Spanish resources shared 22 Jun per Next Step), and aviation vertical is a fit (Zycus aviation collateral shared). Fit is inferred, not confirmed.

That reads as functional procurement modernization rather than AI-first transformation. Merlin, ANA and Agentic AI are tagged but appear to be vendor positioning rather than buyer-requested.
• There are zero Avoma calls at opportunity or account level across the date range. Confidence is low because there is no recorded buyer voice.

deltas.to: Deal is cold (45 days since last touchpoint, 144+ days in Formal Evaluation with no milestone progression). Rep outreach 22 Jun awaits buyer reply. Immediate le
deltas.subject: Deal is cold (45 days since last touchpoint, 144+ days in Formal Evaluation with no milestone progression). Rep outreach 22 Jun awaits buyer reply. Immediate lever: restart relationship with high-value soft touch (Spanish aviation ROI case study or Merlin demo video) within 7 days to re-engage the relevant stakeholder and SVP Supply Chain, then on first live call ask who internally is driving this initiative and secure C-level intro.

deltas.to: Competing finance transformation initiative may be deprioritizing procurement workstream and starving it of budget/exec attention per Next Step 22 Jun. Procurem
deltas.subject: Competing finance transformation initiative may be deprioritizing procurement workstream and starving it of budget/exec attention per Next Step 22 Jun. Procurement vs finance prioritization is unclear. Win lever: on next live call ask the relevant stakeholder directly whether procurement initiative is alive, what the budget/timeline is, and how it relates to the finance transformation (parallel, sequential, or competing for same dollars). If procurement is deprioritized, qualify out or defer to 2027.

deltas.to: Single-thread risk and no C-level sponsor. Only 2 buyer contacts (VP Planning, SVP Supply Chain, both operational), no CFO/COO/CTO/Digital Transformation exec e
deltas.subject: Single-thread risk and no C-level sponsor. Only 2 buyer contacts (VP Planning, SVP Supply Chain, both operational), no CFO/COO/CTO/Digital Transformation exec engaged. Account contact list shows CTO Alicia Berbel, COO Santiago Diago, and Digital Transformation leaders (Greysi Reyes Arreguin Senior Director, Sergio Balladares Sr Manager, Jaqueline Granados Senior Program Manager) who are not mapped to this opp. Win lever: ask the relevant stakeholder for intro to the C-level sponsor who will approve $200k investment and to the relevant stakeholder lead who owns the transformation roadmap, then multi-thread to them within 14 days.

RUN DISCOVERY TO the relevant stakeholder CASE: 144+ days in Formal Evaluation with zero discovery calls, no pain/metrics/criteria documented, and no champion developed. The deal has product scope but no validated need. Within the next 14 days, run a discovery/scoping call (ideally with the relevant stakeholderña and VP Erika Baños together, or separately if needed) to capture: the top 3 procurement pain points, current-state cost/risk baseline, what success looks like in 12 months, the decision timeline and approval chain, who else is being evaluated, and why now (compelling event). Use the aviation-specific and Spanish-language collateral already shared (per Next Step 22 Jun) as conversation anchors. Without a quantified pain and a named champion, this deal will not progress.

deltas.to: IMMEDIATE RE-ENGAGEMENT WITH the relevant stakeholder: 4 days since rep outreach (22 Jun follow-up to the relevant stakeholderños, direct reach to the relevant
deltas.subject: IMMEDIATE RE-ENGAGEMENT WITH the relevant stakeholder: 4 days since rep outreach (22 Jun follow-up to the relevant stakeholderños, direct reach to the relevant stakeholderña) with no confirmed reply. Limited client responsiveness is flagged as a risk. The highest-leverage move is to secure a live call with the relevant stakeholder (the likely budget authority) in the next 7 days to extract pain, timeline, and decision process, converting a cold email thread into an active relationship. If no reply by 03 Jul (10 business days), escalate through EY to request a joint client meeting and assess whether the deal is truly live or should be disqualified.

deltas.to: CLARIFY EY ROLE AND PRIORITIZATION: Next Step notes EY was engaged 22 Jun to understand whether Aeroméxico's ongoing finance transformation is delaying the proc
deltas.subject: CLARIFY EY ROLE AND PRIORITIZATION: Next Step notes EY was engaged 22 Jun to understand whether Aeroméxico's ongoing finance transformation is delaying the procurement workstream. This is critical path: if procurement is deprioritized or budget-starved by the finance initiative, the 2026-12-31 close is fiction. Within the next 14 days, align with EY on: (1) Aeroméxico's current initiative prioritization and budget allocation, (2) EY's implementation timeline and SOW scoping status, (3) whether EY holds decision influence or is pure implementation overlay, and (4) EY's view on competitive landscape (sole-source vs RFP). Use EY as a forcing function to get in front of the Economic Buyer if direct outreach continues to stall.
